An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Dennis, your responses indicate that your readiness to follow opportunity to another employer or another location may help you to get ahead. Your ability to adapt your behavior to suit a variety of roles and circumstances suggest that you have a wide range of professional options open to you. You are more active than most people in searching for information about potential employers, and in assessing your own interests and abilities. Your skill in conflict management is rooted in the respect others have for your integrity, and in your own desire for harmony.
You are more likely to resolve conflicts through collaboration and compromise. You place a high importance on the image you project to others.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Your responses indicate that you would probably perform well if your environment suddenly became more challenging.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Reflector. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Dennis, your responses indicate that you are an exceptionally patient observer of people and things. You have an impartial, careful, and contemplative approach to learning that relies heavily on your intuitive perceptions about human nature. You enjoy quietly pondering ideas and concepts, and you always seek fair solutions. Before forming an opinion, you consider all of the information available, you take your time, and you do not like to be rushed.

You tend to prefer learning situations that allow you to be an objective observer. You prefer that lecturers focus primarily on integrative conceptual models, and not on conflict and controversy. During group discussions, it is likely that you express your opinions diplomatically. In the classroom, you probably tend to keep a relatively low profile. You listen closely to what other people say, and you can probably detect meaning in their vocal tone and body language, as well.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Dennis, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Jeff, your responses indicate that you are very precise, careful and conscientious. Accuracy and excellence are very important to you, and you are cautious of the new until it is proven to be successful. Your strong sense of duty and loyalty is reinforced by your very meticulous approach to life and work. Criticism may readily affect you. If you are called upon to delegate detail work to people, you expect them to perform with speed.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Exceptionally systematic and disciplined
Inspires and challenges others to excellence
Committed to high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Jeff, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in an environment that allows you to develop or use systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project.

You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly altered or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which causes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with intense disapproval; and you may be compelled to counteract by overpowering the opposition with appropriate facts or rules.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Cautious | Self-disciplined | Conservative | Emphasize Quality | Systematic | Status-Conscious | Sensitive To Appraisal | Strict | Loyal | A Perfectionist
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: Since you exhibit a strong sense of duty and loyalty, it follows that you are highly motivated to do what is right. You may be judgmental regarding people who cannot do an activity as well as you can do it. You are especially skillful with detail work, and you fit well in a highly structured organization. You may be inclined to express a rather skeptical reaction toward anything new.

Interaction2.Descriptive Words: Very Reserved | A Selective Communicator | Quiet | Private
Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: It is likely that you are more interested in matters of a technical nature, than in dealing with people-oriented concerns. You are more at ease with people when you really get to know them. You tend to foster close ties to family and a few close friends, and because of this focus, you may appear, at times, to be disinclined toward outsiders.

Interaction3.Descriptive Words: Analytical | Composed | Can Be Abrupt | Modest | Tense | Creative | Like Things Done Quickly
Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You tend to react in an authoritative manner when interacting in a highly structured organizational system. You prefer to tell rather than persuade when communicating with people. Your strong sense of urgency leads you to want activities completed correctly, fairly and as soon as possible.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Conformity, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A structured environment that has few sudden or abrupt changes.
The security of basic benefits.
A worked out system and quality products.
Praise for specific accomplishments.
Standard operating procedures and sound actions.
Fairness in dealings with individuals and groups.
Time to do things right.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You are constantly criticized.
The rules are changed without plenty of notice.
There is a lack of procedures, quality and fairness.
There is not a worked out system.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Dominance trait is lower
There is strong, capable leadership in your environment.
Direction as to what is to be done and when.
A predictable environment that affords a significant amount of protection and peace.
because your Extroversion trait is lower
A limited amount of emotional exposure.
Time alone to think creatively and figure things out.
Respected as a person and taken seriously.
Socializing in a circle where people are known one-on-one.
Time to prepare for group presentations.
because your Conformity trait is higher
A structured environment.
A worked out system for getting the job done.
Security, training and quality products.

Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Jeff, your responses indicate that you tend to favor the Intuitive approach to decision-making. You approach decision-making with optimism and enthusiasm, and with little anxiety about the possibility of making mistakes. You tend to be an energetic leader in the decision-making process, and you are likely to generate a variety of new ideas.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by managing your people through systems and procedures, and requiring them to follow your organization's rules. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while making sure that projects are handled correctly. You show respect for colleagues' positions and you expect the same in return. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to plan and design systems and procedures. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one. You can handle a lot of variety and you like to set a fast pace.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Jeff, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You tend to feel at ease in almost any social situation. You easily build rapport, and it is likely that you are proficient in both maintaining relationships and networking. You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. You are aware of the impact of your emotions on others, and you are able to attune your own style to others' emotional reactions. You work for reasons beyond money or status.

Stress Level.Intro: Stress is not necessarily all bad; some stress can be healthy. The Stress Level measurement indicates how well you are handling your current environmental demands. The "Good" response indicates that you are coping with your environment effectively, while the other responses indicate varying degrees of difficulty in dealing with it.
Stress Level.Description: Jeff, your responses indicate that during the above-mentioned period, your stress level was Very Stressed. This indicates that you sometimes felt overwhelmed by the challenges in your environment. You may have desired more support from your colleagues, or for more time to get things done.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Energy Level measures capacity for activity, and Energy can be thought of as an individual's "battery power." Energy might be depleted at an increased rate while operating within a stressful environment or in a managerial role. Food, sleep, and relaxation can recharge one's Energy.
When energy runs out, the following symptoms tend to appear:
Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).
Energy Level.Description: Jeff, you currently have a Very High Energy Level. You are highly effective in demanding environments. If called upon, you can maintain your effectiveness in the face of longer hours and stress-related situations. You are also able to juggle a multitude of tasks, or can intensely focus on a single task as necessary.

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Jeff, your responses indicate that you are highly aware of the legal and ethical boundaries concerning business behavior. You ensure that all of your actions are strategically sound, politically appropriate, and tactically feasible. For you to support change, it must be clearly defined and proven constructive. As a manager, you tend to make a great effort to ensure that nothing goes wrong. When faced with unfamiliar circumstances, you tend to rely on proven approaches to see you through.
You tend to act decisively to defend your independence. It is likely that you are less concerned with performance standards imposed by others. You may have a tendency to rebel when others are overly judgmental or critical of your unconventional methods.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Jeff, your responses indicate that you are committed and principled in your approach to work. You are less responsive than other people to shifting environmental demands. You tend to behave consistently even in stressful situations. It may frustrate you that such consistency is not regularly recognized or rewarded.
You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Theorist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Jeff, your responses indicate that you prefer an analytical, conceptual approach to learning. You tend to rely heavily on logical thinking and rational evaluation. You probably tend to be more oriented towards things and symbols, and less towards other people. You learn best in authority-directed, impersonal learning situations that emphasize theory and systematic analysis. You probably do not enjoy unstructured "experiential learning" approaches such as group exercises and simulations.

You are a highly disciplined thinker who sees patterns and connections that other people do not. You take a step-by-step approach to problem solving, and you rely on facts, not feelings, when making decisions. Because abstract conceptualization is a particular strength, you probably find it easy to discern similarities among apparently dissimilar concepts, things, or people. You work hard to minimize uncertainty or ambiguity in your learning environment. Your instructors and fellow learners might regard you as a perfectionist.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Jeff, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Nathan, your responses indicate that you are very precise, persistent, and scholarly. You utilize a systematic approach to problem solving. Since you are concerned with the accuracy of information, you exercise skepticism and caution in regard to matters that have not been proven. Standard rules, organized procedures, and traditional methods are means in which you find comfort and where you can use your good memory and accumulated data. You recognize what is generally accepted as right or wrong and insist that the right course is taken.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Exceptionally systematic and disciplined
Inspires and challenges others to excellence
Committed to high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Nathan, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in an environment that allows you to develop or use systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project.

You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly altered or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which causes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with intense disapproval; and you may be compelled to counteract by overpowering the opposition with appropriate facts or rules.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Traditional | Self-critical | Conscientious | Emphasize Quality | Accurate | Loyal | Cautious | Skillful With Facts | Concerned | Perfectionist | Intensely Aware
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: Your valuable ability to function within a defined system, which is worked out in detailed steps, leads you to demand that the details are dealt with properly. You may suspend judgment regarding new programs and projects, until you are convinced that their features will prove to be workable and useful. It is likely that you discourage gossipy discussions, uninformed arguments and demotivating criticism. You appreciate receiving specific praise, especially if it is handled in a sensitive manner that does not cause undue embarrassment. You prefer to deal with fair and strong leadership in your work environment.

Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: You are drawn to situations where you are made to feel that you belong. You express yourself in a genuine and factual manner. It is possible that you possess a witty and ironic sense of humor.

Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You tend to be direct in your communications. You are goal-oriented, imaginative, creative and a good organizer.

Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: You are able to pace yourself to a time frame within an activity. You think through the consequences of your actions. Your approach to problem solving is very methodical and deliberate.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Conformity, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A structured environment that has few sudden or abrupt changes.
The security of basic benefits.
A worked out system and quality products.
Praise for specific accomplishments.
Standard operating procedures and sound actions.
Fairness in dealings with individuals and groups.
Time to do things right.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You are constantly criticized.
The rules are changed without plenty of notice.
There is a lack of procedures, quality and fairness.
There is not a worked out system.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Dominance trait is higher
Daily challenges.
Results-oriented approach to project.
Direct, to-the-point communications.
because your Extroversion trait is lower
A limited amount of emotional exposure.
Time alone to think creatively and figure things out.
Respected as a person and taken seriously.
Socializing in a circle where people are known one-on-one.
Time to prepare for group presentations.
because your Conformity trait is higher
A structured environment.
A worked out system for getting the job done.
Security, training and quality products.

Decision Making Style.Intro:
How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Nathan, your responses indicate that you tend to favor the Rational approach to decision-making. You approach the decision-making process carefully, and with a desire to avoid making mistakes. It is likely that you attempt to make decisions only after considering all the facts.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by demanding that your people follow your organization's rules and procedures. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while applying yourself to directing systems and procedures. You are very intent upon having projects completed correctly. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to be satisfied that goals are being reached properly. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Nathan, your responses indicate that you tend to pursue your goals energetically, and that you persevere until your goals are achieved. You strive continually for self-improvement, and you are not discouraged by temporary setbacks or disappointments. You perform your work conscientiously, and delay gratification while pursuing your goals. You tend to control or redirect any of your negative impulses or moods, and you form carefully considered judgments about people or situations.

Criteria1.Title: Personal Insight
Criteria1.Intro: The capacity to accurately recognize and understand own emotions as they occur & to base personal self-confidence on an accurate assessment of own abilities.

Criteria2.Title: Self-Discipline
Criteria2.Intro: The capacity to delay gratification when pursuing goals, to speak carefully, & to control negative impulses.

Criteria3.Title: Drive
Criteria3.Intro: The capacity to pursue goals energetically, to relentlessly seek self-improvement, & to persevere despite obstacles or disappointments.

Criteria4.Title: Interpersonal Insight
Criteria4.Intro: The capacity to understand the emotions of others, to adjust own style to interact well with others, & to empathize with alternative perspectives.

Criteria5.Title: Social Agility
Criteria5.Intro: The capacity to be at ease in almost any social situation, to prevent or resolve conflict, to cultivate rapport & build lasting relationships.

Profile Report.Conscientiousness: You balance solid, goal-oriented achievement and a relaxed approach to life. You may fluctuate between periods of highly motivated, achievement-oriented activity and periods of more easy-going behavior. Alternatively, you may demonstrate a sustained, moderate level of effort toward your goals. While you may not demonstrate an intense desire for achievement for its own sake, you are able to summon a sense of purposefulness and self-discipline when needed. The level of your positive motivation toward achievement may depend on the task or situation.

The Movement of Traits.Description: Nathan, your responses indicate how you have been moving your traits in order to adjust to the environmental pressures of your job. These adjustments are from the recent past, usually from 4 to 8 weeks prior to taking the survey. Research shows that individuals alter their Environmental/Role Adjustments at least every 3 to 4 months. Please see the Role Adjustment Profile for monitoring this trait movement activity.

Adjustment1.Description: This indicates that one of two changes is happening to you. Either there has been some disappointment (or let down) by a person or persons; or it has become necessary for you to spend more time alone figuring things out and doing more detail work than usual.

Adjustment2.Description: This indicates that you are either wanting or being forced to eliminate details or to delegate some details, in order to place emphasis upon a more general, big-picture perspective. Perhaps you are being more open-minded, taking more risks and not performing in as systematic a way as usual.

Environmental/Role Adjustments (ERAs).How Co-Workers See You:
Nathan, your survey responses indicate that you are flexing your behaviors in order to perform your job and/or meet the demands of your environment. Because your on-the-job behaviors are not the same as your Primary Profile, your co-workers may view you quite differently than how you view yourself. The following paragraph describes how your co-workers and manager(s) may see you when you are adjusting to the pressures of your job:



Nathan, your co-workers may observe that you are very patient, steady, cooperative and dependable. You take pride in your work, show self-confidence in your abilities, and are able to improve the processes of routine operations. If you are called upon to improvise with equipment or think of time saving methods, you are capable of performing these functions. Reliable systems that have proved successful in past experiences are your preference. While you are effective at setting work schedule priorities, you are demotivated when there are too many projects in progress at one time.

When faced with change, you excel at instituting new rules for the proper conduct of business. You would prefer to shape the competitive landscape, rather than merely react to the moves of others. By focusing on quality, efficiency, and consistency, you create demand for your organization's products and services.
You are always looking for a better way of doing things. Traditional approaches, even if proven and efficient, do not appeal to you. You enjoy being a pioneer in your field or industry.

Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Reflector. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Nathan, your responses indicate that you are an exceptionally disciplined observer of people and things. You have an impartial, careful, and precise approach to learning that relies heavily on your finely tuned perception. You enjoy thinking about ideas and concepts, and about how they relate to what you already know. Before forming an opinion, you thoroughly analyze all of the information available, you take your time, and you proceed in an orderly fashion.

You tend to prefer learning situations that allow you to be an objective observer. You prefer that lecturers not stray too far from the planned agenda. During group discussions, you may sometimes say only what is expected, rather than what you really think. In the classroom, you probably tend to keep a relatively low profile. You listen closely to what other people say, although you may silently form objections if their viewpoint is different from your own.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Nathan, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Pragmatist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should take advantage of any opportunity to engage in experiments or group discussions. When learning a new idea or concept, think of practical applications for this knowledge. Try to see the opportunities inherent in every problem. Remember that work expands to fill the time allotted for it; make a realistic work schedule and stick to it. When asking questions or participating in group discussions, get straight to the point.

Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Mark, your responses indicate that you communicate directly, take risks without hesitation, and plan out your objectives. Your very independent, persistent and creative nature often leads you to cultivate concepts that create fundamental changes within your sphere of influence. Due to your constant self-determination, you remain true to your goal until you reach it.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Decisive, contemplative, direct and confident
Assertive self-starter
Generates innovative, creative ideas
Able to see the whole picture
Risk taker
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Mark, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a self-motivated and inventive professional, who is able to work independently; you are an analytical and imaginative thinker, who shows strength in recognizing and solving problems; and you thrive in a serious work environment that allows you to be goal-oriented, resourceful, and critical of mediocrity and indecisiveness.

You express your earnest drive to get things done through a venturesome sphere of action, and you may commit yourself to achieving results above and beyond what is expected.

Your strong personality and your selective choice of words may strike others as rather authoritative. You are a keen advocate of less talk and more action. You appreciate candor and positive productivity in your relations with co-workers. If a project should slip off the course that you planned, you implement new decisions to alter the course and to keep the control, which is essential to your disposition.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: When people attempt to wield control over you or they interfere with your progress.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When someone forces you into a corner, you do not hesitate to exert your dominance in order to gain control of the situation.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Creative | Strong-willed | Analytical | Highly Technical | Can Be Very Direct | Conceptual
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: You are the type of person who believes in yourself and what you can do. You respect authority and organizational purpose if they are both oriented toward achieving results. You feel that you can accomplish all challenges that are presented to you.

Interaction2.Descriptive Words: Less Care To Detail | Free Thinker | Disciplined Only To Personal Desires | Contrary | Very Self-confident | Big-picture-oriented
Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: Rigid rules are too restrictive to you. Too much detail work suppresses your imagination. You conceive ideas ahead of their time, and you may be given to express extraordinary ideas. You take risks, especially in your area of expertise.

Interaction3.Descriptive Words: Casual | Unhurried | Persevering | Persistent | Enduring | Hard To Rush | Determined
Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You express a take it or leave it attitude at times, and you refuse to change your mind unless you prove yourself wrong. You perform activities repeatedly in order to improve efficiency. It is likely that you are tuned in to nature.

Interaction4.Descriptive Words: Very Creative | Reserved Socially | Individualistic | Selective Communicator | Private
Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: You may tend to disregard people's opinions. At times you may prefer solitude.

Because your High Trait is Dominance, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
Daily challenges.
Tangible results.
A position with power and prestige.
Direct answers and candor in communications.
The respect of the leadership.
A generous amount of freedom from controls, constant supervision and details.
The ability to measure results on a regular basis in monetary terms, (keep score).
Opportunities to be in charge, make decisions and be responsible for the results achieved.

Not challenged.
Supervised too closely.
You receive vague answers to questions.

A limited amount of emotional exposure.

Respected as a person and taken seriously.

because your Patience trait is higher
A stable and harmonious working environment.
A minimum of personality conflicts.
Adequate time to adjust.
A limited number of last minute time pressures.
because your Conformity trait is lower
Freedom from rules, details, and reports.
A generous amount of independence and unusual assignments.
New methods of doing things away from tradition.

You tend to take on additional responsibilities and may be disinclined to delegate authority. You actively promote change while looking for new methods to achieve goals. You prefer to create an environment where there is harmony and a cooperative effort.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Mark, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You tend to feel at ease in almost any social situation. You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. You are aware of the impact of your emotions on others, and you are able to attune your own style to others' emotional reactions. You work for reasons beyond money or status.

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Mark, your responses indicate that you enjoy taking initiative at work. You are not satisfied until you have achieved tangible results. You consistently push yourself to exceed ordinary performance standards, and to have a significant impact on organizations, people, or situations. It is likely that your perseverance often results in transformational change for both you and your organization. Your professional accomplishments are likely to have wide-ranging impact.
You are highly skilled at anticipating and preventing problems. You prefer to have some general guidelines to follow when taking action. Given proper support, you will work hard to find new ways to add value for your organization.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Mark, your responses indicate that you insist on being true to yourself despite social expectations. Your behavior is governed by your deeply held personal values. It is likely that you would not compromise your principles for the sake of advancement. You are more likely than most people to resign from an organization because of philosophical differences.
You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.
You are not making significant behavioral adjustments in response to the demands of your current environment. This may mean that you do not consider your current environment particularly challenging, or that your current position constitutes a "natural fit" for you.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Pragmatist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Mark, your responses indicate that you prefer a "hands-on" approach to learning involving active experimentation. You learn best by engaging in homework, projects, or face-to-face conversations with authorities. You tend to dislike passive learning situations such as lectures. Because you learn best by doing, not by watching or listening, it is likely that you thoroughly practice new skills until you have mastered them.

You enjoy testing theories to see if they work in the "real world"; and, you consistently think of better, more practical ways to get things done. You see problems as opportunities, and you frequently solve problems by trial and error. In discussions, you tend to get straight to the point; and, you prefer that discussions have an agenda and a time limit. You probably spend less time than others preparing for classes or examinations.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Mark, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Theorist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should adopt the attitude that most things are more alike than they are dissimilar. Question whether or not you are thinking logically, or if you are relying overly much on your "gut feelings." Approach problem solving in a step-by-step manner. Make an effort to separate people and their personalities from the intellectual ideas and concepts they profess. If you are unclear about the details of a concept, ask questions or do additional research until the ambiguity is resolved. Focus on the distinction between facts and opinions.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Tactile Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Kenneth, your responses indicate that you are very precise, careful and conscientious. Accuracy and excellence are very important to you, and you are cautious of the new until it is proven to be successful. Your strong sense of duty and loyalty is reinforced by your very meticulous approach to life and work. Criticism may readily affect you. If you are called upon to delegate detail work to people, you expect them to perform with speed.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Exceptionally systematic and disciplined
Inspires and challenges others to excellence
Committed to high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Kenneth, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in an environment that allows you to develop or use systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project.

You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly altered or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which causes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with intense disapproval; and you may be compelled to counteract by overpowering the opposition with appropriate facts or rules.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Cautious | Self-disciplined | Conservative | Emphasize Quality | Systematic | Status-Conscious | Sensitive To Appraisal | Strict | Loyal | A Perfectionist
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: Since you exhibit a strong sense of duty and loyalty, it follows that you are highly motivated to do what is right. You may be judgmental regarding people who cannot do an activity as well as you can do it. You are especially skillful with detail work, and you fit well in a highly structured organization. You may be inclined to express a rather skeptical reaction toward anything new.

Interaction2.Descriptive Words: Very Reserved | A Selective Communicator | Quiet | Private
Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: It is likely that you are more interested in matters of a technical nature, than in dealing with people-oriented concerns. You are more at ease with people when you really get to know them. You tend to foster close ties to family and a few close friends, and because of this focus, you may appear, at times, to be disinclined toward outsiders.

Interaction3.Descriptive Words: Analytical | Composed | Can Be Abrupt | Modest | Tense | Creative | Like Things Done Quickly
Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You tend to react in an authoritative manner when interacting in a highly structured organizational system. You prefer to tell rather than persuade when communicating with people. Your strong sense of urgency leads you to want activities completed correctly, fairly and as soon as possible.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Conformity, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A structured environment that has few sudden or abrupt changes.
The security of basic benefits.
A worked out system and quality products.
Praise for specific accomplishments.
Standard operating procedures and sound actions.
Fairness in dealings with individuals and groups.
Time to do things right.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You are constantly criticized.
The rules are changed without plenty of notice.
There is a lack of procedures, quality and fairness.
There is not a worked out system.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Extroversion trait is lower
A limited amount of emotional exposure.
Time alone to think creatively and figure things out.
Respected as a person and taken seriously.
Socializing in a circle where people are known one-on-one.
Time to prepare for group presentations.
because your Patience trait is lower
A fast pace with a lot of variety.
Freedom from routine.
New environments in which to work or play.
Assignments that require quick action.
because your Conformity trait is higher
A structured environment.
A worked out system for getting the job done.
Security, training and quality products.

Decision Making Style.Intro:
How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Kenneth, your responses indicate that you tend to favor the Rational approach to decision-making. You approach the decision-making process carefully, capably, and calmly. It is likely that your skill at organization enables you to conduct a steady, thorough analysis of any problem.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by managing your people through systems and procedures, and requiring them to follow your organization's rules. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while making sure that projects are handled correctly. You show respect for colleagues' positions and you expect the same in return. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to plan and design systems and procedures. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one. You can handle a lot of variety and you like to set a fast pace.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Kenneth, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You tend to control or redirect any of your negative impulses or moods. You perform your work conscientiously, and delay gratification while pursuing your goals. You perform accurate self-appraisals of your talents and abilities. You are able to attune your own style to the emotional reactions of others. You strive continually for self-improvement. You do not allow negative emotions to interfere with your social interactions.

Criteria1.Title: Personal Insight
Criteria1.Intro: The capacity to accurately recognize and understand own emotions as they occur & to base personal self-confidence on an accurate assessment of own abilities.

Criteria2.Title: Self-Discipline
Criteria2.Intro: The capacity to delay gratification when pursuing goals, to speak carefully, & to control negative impulses.

Criteria3.Title: Drive
Criteria3.Intro: The capacity to pursue goals energetically, to relentlessly seek self-improvement, & to persevere despite obstacles or disappointments.

Criteria4.Title: Interpersonal Insight
Criteria4.Intro: The capacity to understand the emotions of others, to adjust own style to interact well with others, & to empathize with alternative perspectives.

Criteria5.Title: Social Agility
Criteria5.Intro: The capacity to be at ease in almost any social situation, to prevent or resolve conflict, to cultivate rapport & build lasting relationships.

Profile Report.Conscientiousness: You work hard, demonstrate competence and are determined to fulfill your goals. Your efforts are effective, and your methods are usually unassuming and considerate. Your positive motivations, combined with discipline, planning and purposefulness, lead you to fulfill your tasks and obligations with excellence in mind.

The Movement of Traits.Description: Kenneth, your responses indicate how you have been moving your traits in order to adjust to the environmental pressures of your job. These adjustments are from the recent past, usually from 4 to 8 weeks prior to taking the survey. Research shows that individuals alter their Environmental/Role Adjustments at least every 3 to 4 months. Please see the Role Adjustment Profile for monitoring this trait movement activity.

Adjustment1.Description: This indicates that you are exerting more control, assertiveness and forcefulness than is natural for you. This might be caused by the pressures in your environment.

Adjustment2.Description: This indicates that you are making an effort to be more friendly, empathetic and sensitive in terms of people. It is possible that you are being more communicative in general, or are working to develop more people skills.

Adjustment3.Description: This indicates that events are not happening as quickly as you desire. Perhaps deadlines are not being met, and your sense of urgency is increasing with a need to move at a faster pace.

Adjustment4.Description: This indicates that you are either wanting or being forced to eliminate details or to delegate some details, in order to place emphasis upon a more general, big-picture perspective. Perhaps you are being more open-minded, taking more risks and not performing in as systematic a way as usual.

Environmental/Role Adjustments (ERAs).How Co-Workers See You:
Kenneth, your survey responses indicate that you are flexing your behaviors in order to perform your job and/or meet the demands of your environment. Because your on-the-job behaviors are not the same as your Primary Profile, your co-workers may view you quite differently than how you view yourself. The following paragraph describes how your co-workers and manager(s) may see you when you are adjusting to the pressures of your job:



Kenneth, your co-workers may observe that you require projects and tasks to be completed quickly and correctly. You are conscientious about your work and take an active role in correcting mistakes and problems. When you are critical of others, you try not to demoralize them. Your strong sense of urgency leads you to be involved in many projects at the same time. You are friendly and convincing, yet strict regarding rules and standards.

Stress Level.Intro: Stress is not necessarily all bad; some stress can be healthy. The Stress Level measurement indicates how well you are handling your current environmental demands. The "Good" response indicates that you are coping with your environment effectively, while the other responses indicate varying degrees of difficulty in dealing with it.
Stress Level.Description: Kenneth, your responses indicate that during the above-mentioned period, your stress level was Very Stressed. This indicates that you sometimes felt overwhelmed by the challenges in your environment. You may have desired more support from your colleagues, or for more time to get things done.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Energy Level measures capacity for activity, and Energy can be thought of as an individual's "battery power." Energy might be depleted at an increased rate while operating within a stressful environment or in a managerial role. Food, sleep, and relaxation can recharge one's Energy.
When energy runs out, the following symptoms tend to appear:
Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).
Energy Level.Description: Kenneth, you currently have a Very High Energy Level. You are highly effective in demanding environments. If called upon, you can maintain your effectiveness in the face of longer hours and stress-related situations. You are also able to juggle a multitude of tasks, or can intensely focus on a single task as necessary.

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Kenneth, your responses indicate that you are dedicated to changing things, in an intended direction, for the better. You are highly skilled at risk-assessment, but you are not afraid of taking chances, given the appropriate benefit incentive. When faced with change, you excel at instituting new rules for the proper conduct of business. You would prefer to shape the competitive landscape, rather than merely react to the moves of others. By focusing on quality, efficiency, and consistency, you create demand for your organization's products and services.
You are always looking for a better way of doing things. Traditional approaches, even if proven and efficient, do not appeal to you. You enjoy being a pioneer in your field or industry.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Kenneth, your responses indicate that you are committed and principled in your approach to work. You are less responsive than other people to shifting environmental demands. You tend to behave consistently even in stressful situations. It may frustrate you that such consistency is not regularly recognized or rewarded.
You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Theorist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Kenneth, your responses indicate that you prefer an analytical, conceptual approach to learning. You tend to rely heavily on logical thinking and rational evaluation. You probably tend to be more oriented towards things and symbols, and less towards other people. You learn best in authority-directed, impersonal learning situations that emphasize theory and systematic analysis. You probably do not enjoy unstructured "experiential learning" approaches such as group exercises and simulations.

You are a highly disciplined thinker who sees patterns and connections that other people do not. You take a step-by-step approach to problem solving, and you rely on facts, not feelings, when making decisions. Because abstract conceptualization is a particular strength, you probably find it easy to discern similarities among apparently dissimilar concepts, things, or people. You work hard to minimize uncertainty or ambiguity in your learning environment. Your instructors and fellow learners might regard you as a perfectionist.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Kenneth, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Michael, your responses indicate that you are very patient, steady, cooperative and dependable. You take pride in your work, show self-confidence in your abilities, and are able to improve the processes of routine operations. If you are called upon to improvise with equipment or think of time saving methods, you are capable of performing these functions. Reliable systems that have proved successful in past experiences are your preference. While you are effective at setting work schedule priorities, you are demotivated when there are too many projects in progress at one time.

High Trait.Description: Pace And Rate Of Motion Trait
High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Highly motivated to promote a productive, positive work environment
Perceptive listener
Exceptionally dependable and persistent
Able to balance task needs with employee needs
Effective in balancing warmth with professionalism
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Michael, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a collaborative professional, who is inclined to be highly effectual in dealing with routine and in analyzing work flow and communication patterns, in order to maximize the effectiveness of your work team.

You may express your cooperative and supportive disposition by assisting in the successful resolution of conflicts, by promoting harmonious relationships within your organization, and by assessing co-workers' needs carefully.

You thrive in a goal-oriented environment that encourages you to produce at a measured and steady pace, that allows you to direct your energies toward a fixed number of projects to be completed in realistic time frames, and that recognizes your competitive qualities are aimed at realizing success not only for yourself but for your organization.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: Undue pressure that is applied without justification; and a bombardment of diverse projects that need to be completed at once.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: In order to avoid confrontation, you may appear to accept unfavorable conditions imposed upon you. However, after an appropriate interlude, you will work quietly to restore balance to the situation.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Conformity, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Patient | Easygoing | Good Memory | Stable | Learn Thoroughly | Unhurried | Casual | Cooperative | Avoid Conflict
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: You understand how to establish a steady rate of performance for yourself. Since you are skillful with projects that require repetitive action over long periods, it follows that you are capable of improving upon time and motion efficiency. You recognize the value of quantity versus quality when speed is necessary. Your reasonable nature assists you in blending well with people in your environment. You accept life without feeling compelled to constantly change it.

Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: You do not mind working alone, in fact, sometimes you may prefer to work alone. You favor one-on-one relationships and smaller groups. You are a selective communicator.

Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You prove to be most effective with a detailed plan of action. You react sensitively to criticism, and you are watchful about new and unproven concepts. You work well within a thought-out system.

Interaction4.Descriptive Words: Direct In Communication | Modest | Analytical | Mechanically Innovative | Creative
Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: If you would be called upon to act in a supervisory role, you would tend to communicate as a teller rather than as a persuader. You rely heavily on what has been proven to work in the past. You are innovative in your area of expertise, and it is possible that you possess strong mechanical dexterity.

Because your High Trait is Patience, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
An environment where there is harmony and cooperation.
A minimum of conflicts and a steady, stable structure.
A pace set for you with no sudden or abrupt changes.
Forewarning of changes so that there is sufficient time to adjust.
Encouragement in regard to creativity.
Knowing how your personal efforts contribute to overall goals.
Validation of self-worth.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
Constantly pressured at the last minute.
There are too many personality conflicts.
There are too many sudden changes.
Expectations are too high and/or not clear.

There is strong, capable leadership in your environment.
Direction as to what is to be done and when.

You tend to delegate authority and may be disinclined to delegate details. You require time to be alone and think matters through in order to be sure that projects are proceeding smoothly.

You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. You are aware of the impact of your emotions on others, and you are able to attune your own style to others' emotional reactions. You work for reasons beyond money or status.
