Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Michael, your responses indicate that you are more likely to be involved in activities aimed at improving the lives of other people. Your commitment to helping others may have led you to become a corporate, civic, or community leader. It is very likely that you regularly set effective, change-oriented goals. Efficient scheduling and effective allocation of resources are some of your strongest skills. You fear complacency and continually strive to improve your professional performance.
You have a strong "bias for action." Learning by doing works best for you. It is unlikely than you would abandon an idea before trying to implement it in the real world.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Michael, your responses indicate that you value principles over pragmatism. It is important to you that your career is aligned with your personal beliefs. Your behavior at work usually reflects your deeply held values. You value honesty, and you are sometimes surprised by the dishonesty of others.
You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Theorist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Michael, your responses indicate that you prefer an abstract, conceptual approach to learning. You tend to make good use of logical thinking and rational evaluation. You probably tend to be more oriented towards symbolism, and less towards concrete facts. You learn best from experts, and in learning situations that emphasize theories and systems. You probably do not enjoy unstructured "experiential learning" approaches such as group exercises and simulations.

You are a highly intuitive thinker who sees patterns and connections that other people do not. You plan before tackling tough problems, and you work hard to ensure that facts, not feelings, inform your decisions. Because abstract conceptualization is a particular strength, you probably find it easy to discern similarities among apparently dissimilar concepts, things, or people. You are comfortable with uncertainty or ambiguity, but your natural insight tends to limit their influence in your learning environment. Your instructors and fellow learners might regard you as a highly integrated thinker.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Michael, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Pragmatist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should take advantage of any opportunity to engage in experiments or group discussions. When learning a new idea or concept, think of practical applications for this knowledge. Try to see the opportunities inherent in every problem. Remember that work expands to fill the time allotted for it; make a realistic work schedule and stick to it. When asking questions or participating in group discussions, get straight to the point.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Maurice, your responses indicate that you are very precise, persistent, and scholarly. You utilize a systematic approach to problem solving. Since you are concerned with the accuracy of information, you exercise skepticism and caution in regard to matters that have not been proven. Standard rules, organized procedures, and traditional methods are means in which you find comfort and where you can use your good memory and accumulated data. You recognize what is generally accepted as right or wrong and insist that the right course is taken.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Exceptionally committed, creative, and systematic
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Motivates others to excellence
Supports high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Maurice, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and trustworthy professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in a serious environment that allows you to invent or utilize systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project. You may show reserved sensitivity and responsiveness to motivating praise and demotivating criticism.

Due to your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles involved are fair to all concerned.

Because you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and you like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to carefully explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make unwanted changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly changed or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which provokes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with guarded disapproval; and you may be compelled to relate a carefully selected choice of facts or rules in order to counteract the issue or criticism.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Traditional | Self-critical | Conscientious | Emphasize Quality | Accurate | Loyal | Cautious | Skillful With Facts | Concerned | Perfectionist | Intensely Aware
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: Your valuable ability to function within a defined system, which is worked out in detailed steps, leads you to demand that the details are dealt with properly. You may suspend judgment regarding new programs and projects, until you are convinced that their features will prove to be workable and useful. It is likely that you discourage gossipy discussions, uninformed arguments and demotivating criticism. You appreciate receiving specific praise, especially if it is handled in a sensitive manner that does not cause undue embarrassment. You prefer to deal with fair and strong leadership in your work environment.

Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: You are drawn to situations where you are made to feel that you belong. You express yourself in a genuine and factual manner. It is possible that you possess a witty and ironic sense of humor.

Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You tend to be direct in your communications. You are goal-oriented, imaginative, creative and a good organizer.

Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: You are able to pace yourself to a time frame within an activity. You think through the consequences of your actions. Your approach to problem solving is very methodical and deliberate.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Conformity, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A structured environment that has few sudden or abrupt changes.
The security of basic benefits.
A worked out system and quality products.
Praise for specific accomplishments.
Standard operating procedures and sound actions.
Fairness in dealings with individuals and groups.
Time to do things right.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You are constantly criticized.
The rules are changed without plenty of notice.
There is a lack of procedures, quality and fairness.
There is not a worked out system.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Dominance trait is higher
Daily challenges.
Results-oriented approach to project.
Direct, to-the-point communications.
because your Extroversion trait is lower
A limited amount of emotional exposure.
Time alone to think creatively and figure things out.
Respected as a person and taken seriously.
Socializing in a circle where people are known one-on-one.
Time to prepare for group presentations.
because your Patience trait is higher
A stable and harmonious working environment.
A minimum of personality conflicts.
Adequate time to adjust.
A limited number of last minute time pressures.

Decision Making Style.Intro:
How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Maurice, your responses indicate that you tend to employ a balanced combination of the Rational and Intuitive approaches to decision-making. However, since you place only a moderate emphasis on these two common approaches, it is likely that achieving results and reaching goals are your primary concerns when making decisions.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by demanding that your people follow your organization's rules and procedures. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while applying yourself to directing systems and procedures. You are very intent upon having projects completed correctly. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to be satisfied that goals are being reached properly. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Maurice, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You tend to feel at ease in almost any social situation. You easily build rapport, and it is likely that you are proficient in both maintaining relationships and networking. You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. You are aware of the impact of your emotions on others, and you are able to attune your own style to others' emotional reactions. You work for reasons beyond money or status.

Criteria1.Title: Personal Insight
Criteria1.Intro: The capacity to accurately recognize and understand own emotions as they occur & to base personal self-confidence on an accurate assessment of own abilities.

Criteria2.Title: Self-Discipline
Criteria2.Intro: The capacity to delay gratification when pursuing goals, to speak carefully, & to control negative impulses.

Criteria3.Title: Drive
Criteria3.Intro: The capacity to pursue goals energetically, to relentlessly seek self-improvement, & to persevere despite obstacles or disappointments.

Criteria4.Title: Interpersonal Insight
Criteria4.Intro: The capacity to understand the emotions of others, to adjust own style to interact well with others, & to empathize with alternative perspectives.

Criteria5.Title: Social Agility
Criteria5.Intro: The capacity to be at ease in almost any social situation, to prevent or resolve conflict, to cultivate rapport & build lasting relationships.

Profile Report.Conscientiousness: You balance solid, goal-oriented achievement and a relaxed approach to life. You may fluctuate between periods of highly motivated, achievement-oriented activity and periods of more easy-going behavior. Alternatively, you may demonstrate a sustained, moderate level of effort toward your goals. While you may not demonstrate an intense desire for achievement for its own sake, you are able to summon a sense of purposefulness and self-discipline when needed. The level of your positive motivation toward achievement may depend on the task or situation.

The Movement of Traits.Description: Maurice, your responses indicate how you have been moving your traits in order to adjust to the environmental pressures of your job. These adjustments are from the recent past, usually from 4 to 8 weeks prior to taking the survey. Research shows that individuals alter their Environmental/Role Adjustments at least every 3 to 4 months. Please see the Role Adjustment Profile for monitoring this trait movement activity.

Adjustment1.Description: You are not allowed to be as controlling or decisive as may be desired. Or perhaps you are stepping back and not being as forceful or assertive as would be typical to your nature.

Adjustment2.Description: This indicates that you are needing to slow down and wait for circumstances to happen; or you are trying to be more casual and easygoing, and not pushing as hard as would be typical of you.

Environmental/Role Adjustments (ERAs).How Co-Workers See You:
Maurice, your survey responses indicate that you are flexing your behaviors in order to perform your job and/or meet the demands of your environment. Because your on-the-job behaviors are not the same as your Primary Profile, your co-workers may view you quite differently than how you view yourself. The following paragraph describes how your co-workers and manager(s) may see you when you are adjusting to the pressures of your job:



Maurice, your co-workers may observe that you are very thorough, precise, steady, cooperative and methodical in your approach to life and work. You exercise caution when it pertains to new people, systems and procedures. Your patient, non-rushed and conscientious style is qualified by your insistence that tasks are completed correctly. You are very loyal to people whom you respect. Criticism that is launched in your direction may produce a sensitive reaction on your part.

Stress Level.Intro: Stress is not necessarily all bad; some stress can be healthy. The Stress Level measurement indicates how well you are handling your current environmental demands. The "Good" response indicates that you are coping with your environment effectively, while the other responses indicate varying degrees of difficulty in dealing with it.
Stress Level.Description: Maurice, your responses indicate that during the above-mentioned period, your stress level was Mildly Stressed. This indicates that your environment was of some concern to you, probably because goals were not being achieved quickly enough. It may also indicate that you felt that the rewards gained did not justify the effort expended.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Energy Level measures capacity for activity, and Energy can be thought of as an individual's "battery power." Energy might be depleted at an increased rate while operating within a stressful environment or in a managerial role. Food, sleep, and relaxation can recharge one's Energy.
When energy runs out, the following symptoms tend to appear:
Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).
Energy Level.Description: Maurice, you currently have a Very High Energy Level. You are highly effective in demanding environments. If called upon, you can maintain your effectiveness in the face of longer hours and stress-related situations. You are also able to juggle a multitude of tasks, or can intensely focus on a single task as necessary.

You do not allow your desire to improve existing systems to detract from routine work that must get done, nor do you overstep the bounds of appropriateness in your pursuit of change. You are more likely to be committed to an organization that values and supports your efforts. You are particularly skilled at appraising situations, identifying problems, and allocating resources to find solutions and drive innovation.
You enjoy trying different things, or doing the same things differently. Because you dread the routine and the ordinary, you are probably a consistent champion of change initiatives. Experimentation that leads to innovation is important to you.

Maurice, your responses indicate that you are committed and principled in your approach to work. You are less responsive than other people to shifting environmental demands. You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.

The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:

The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Maurice, your responses indicate that you prefer a "hands-on" approach to learning that includes active experimentation. You learn best by engaging in experiments, homework, or other projects. You tend to dislike passive learning situations such as lectures. Because you learn best by doing, not by watching or listening, it is likely that you thoroughly practice new skills until you have mastered them.

You enjoy testing theories to see if they work in the "real world"; and, you consistently think of better, more practical ways to get things done. Allow some time to elapse before drawing conclusions regarding a new idea or concept. Attend carefully to your scheduling to allow plenty of time for processing new information. During lectures and class discussions, silently paraphrase the ideas of the speaker. See how much you can learn about other people through quiet observation, as opposed to direct inquiry.

Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.

Proactivity.Description:
Danny, your responses indicate that you enjoy working towards the constructive reform of established systems. You operate with your organization's best interests in mind. You do not allow your desire to improve existing systems to detract from routine work that must get done, nor do you overstep the bounds of appropriateness in your pursuit of change. You are more likely to be committed to an organization that values and supports your efforts. You are particularly skilled at appraising situations, identifying problems, and allocating resources to find solutions and drive innovation.
You enjoy trying different things, or doing the same things differently. Because you dread the routine and the ordinary, you are probably a consistent champion of change initiatives. Experimentation that leads to innovation is important to you.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Danny, your responses indicate that you are committed and principled in your approach to work. You are less responsive than other people to shifting environmental demands. You tend to behave consistently even in stressful situations. It may frustrate you that such consistency is not regularly recognized or rewarded.
You prefer to create your own specialized role or niche within an organization. You tend to maintain a firm boundary between your professional life and personal life.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Reflector. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Danny, your responses indicate that you are an exceptionally disciplined observer of people and things. You have an impartial, careful, and precise approach to learning that relies heavily on your finely tuned perception. You enjoy thinking about ideas and concepts, and about how they relate to what you already know. Before forming an opinion, you thoroughly analyze all of the information available, you take your time, and you proceed in an orderly fashion.

You tend to prefer learning situations that allow you to be an objective observer. You prefer that lecturers not stray too far from the planned agenda. During group discussions, you may sometimes say only what is expected, rather than what you really think. In the classroom, you probably tend to keep a relatively low profile. You listen closely to what other people say, although you may silently form objections if their viewpoint is different from your own.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Danny, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Randy, your responses indicate that you are very outgoing, talkative, and people-oriented. You support conformity to organizational standards and structure, and commitment to completing duties correctly. You enjoy working with people and would show interest in such subjects as fair treatment and equity within your workplace. Your strong sense of urgency heightens your self-motivated and enthusiastic style. You are highly effective when you deal with the interfacing of people, systems and details.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Articulate, fast-paced business communicator
Exceptional interpersonal and collaboration skills
Effectual in persuading others through enthusiasm
Highly motivated toward achievement and surpassing prior goals
Able to relate to another person's point-of-view
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Randy, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a self-motivated and action-oriented professional, who thrives on various opportunities involving change and on developing and promoting the visions of others. You are able to think quickly and verbalize spontaneously.

You use your sharp insight into the subtleties of people's personalities, and apply it to motivating your people to move on a project or program with speed.

You enjoy working with people, you are effective in developing staff skills, and your participation in team leadership and efforts tends to inspire and support your co-workers. Your trusting nature shows a great deal of confidence in what your people can accomplish.

Success is the vital ingredient in a corporate atmosphere that drives your spirit and motivates you to activate others.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: Being turned off by someone's explosion of ill will directed squarely at you, especially if it is fired off in front of your colleagues.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: If forced into a conflict, you use your strong communication skills and battle it out with a swift, verbal offensive.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Patience, and Conformity, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Sell Ideas | Motivator | A Good Mixer | Persuasive | Very Outgoing | Optimistic | Stimulating | A Lively Talker
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: You prefer to be in a business that is related to people. Your intuitive nature assists you well in the accomplishment of projects through teamwork. You are very capable of selling other people's ideas. If called upon to develop and train people, you accomplish this with confidence. You desire that opportunities are made available to you, so it is likely that you focus on being a part of an expanding organization.

Interaction2.Descriptive Words: Flexible And Quick | Action-oriented | Fast | A Hard Driver
Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: You tend to be a fluent and fast communicator who is always on the move. Your very strong sense of urgency allows you to be highly competitive and ambitious.

Interaction3.Descriptive Words: Poised | An Organizer | Self-confident | Self-starting | Positive | Forceful | Independent
Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You produce a strong impact upon people. You respond positively to a results-oriented approach to a project. If it is possible, you endeavor to surround yourself with strong people who may possibly help you to elevate your own position. Your curious and inquisitive mind serves you in understanding human nature.

Interaction4.Descriptive Words: Good Trainer | Not Easygoing | A Perfectionist | A Good Teacher | Loyal | Emphasizes Quality | Strict | Conscientious
Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: You are skillful with detail and show a strong drive to accomplish activities accurately and in their proper priority, especially as they relate to people. You follow directions carefully, and you prefer to perform duties in the same manner that they have proven to work in the past.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Extroversion, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A lot of interaction with people.
Meeting new people and making friends.
Opportunities to make more money and improve status.
Being a team player within the organization.
Praise and public recognition.
Identifying with an organization that has prestige and a good public image.
Awareness of what is going on in the organization.
Acceptance and being liked by others.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You perceive you are not liked.
You are not invited into meetings with your peers.
Your territory, (opportunity) is reduced in size.
You feel you are not part of the team.
You do not have enough people contact.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Dominance trait is higher
Daily challenges.
Results-oriented approach to project.
Direct, to-the-point communications.
because your Extroversion trait is higher
Opportunities for people-interaction.
Meeting new people and making friends.
The team approach to getting things done.
Opportunities for success and status.
because your Conformity trait is higher
A structured environment.
A worked out system for getting the job done.
Security, training and quality products.

Decision Making Style.Intro:
How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Randy, your responses indicate that you tend to employ a balanced combination of the Rational and Intuitive approaches to decision-making. However, since you place only a moderate emphasis on these two common approaches, it is likely that achieving results and reaching goals are your primary concerns when making decisions.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You perform your leadership role by using your exceptional ability to interpret people's actions and dialogue, and then by persuading them to do things your way. You like a leadership role, function in a manner suitable to your environment, and support teamwork. You develop your people with enthusiasm and want quality results. You may be disinclined to delegate details and authority. You prefer to deal with systems and procedures that have worked in the past.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Randy, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is well developed. You work for reasons beyond money or status. It is likely that you are proficient in both maintaining relationships and networking. You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. Furthermore, you are aware of the impact of your emotions on others.

Criteria1.Title: Personal Insight
Criteria1.Intro: The capacity to accurately recognize and understand own emotions as they occur & to base personal self-confidence on an accurate assessment of own abilities.

Criteria2.Title: Self-Discipline
Criteria2.Intro: The capacity to delay gratification when pursuing goals, to speak carefully, & to control negative impulses.

Criteria3.Title: Drive
Criteria3.Intro: The capacity to pursue goals energetically, to relentlessly seek self-improvement, & to persevere despite obstacles or disappointments.

Criteria4.Title: Interpersonal Insight
Criteria4.Intro: The capacity to understand the emotions of others, to adjust own style to interact well with others, & to empathize with alternative perspectives.

Criteria5.Title: Social Agility
Criteria5.Intro: The capacity to be at ease in almost any social situation, to prevent or resolve conflict, to cultivate rapport & build lasting relationships.

Profile Report.Conscientiousness: You are self-disciplined, achievement-oriented, and motivated toward your goals. You are purposeful, thorough, and organized. You are planful and task-oriented, and you work hard to fulfill your obligations. You are competent and determined, and you strive for perfection. Your emotions tend to provide positive rather than negative motivation in getting things done.

The Movement of Traits.Description: Randy, your responses indicate how you have been moving your traits in order to adjust to the environmental pressures of your job. These adjustments are from the recent past, usually from 4 to 8 weeks prior to taking the survey. Research shows that individuals alter their Environmental/Role Adjustments at least every 3 to 4 months. Please see the Role Adjustment Profile for monitoring this trait movement activity.

Adjustment1.Description: You are not allowed to be as controlling or decisive as may be desired. Or perhaps you are stepping back and not being as forceful or assertive as would be typical to your nature.

Adjustment2.Description: This indicates that one of two changes is happening to you. Either there has been some disappointment (or let down) by a person or persons; or it has become necessary for you to spend more time alone figuring things out and doing more detail work than usual.

Adjustment3.Description: This indicates that you are needing to slow down and wait for circumstances to happen; or you are trying to be more casual and easygoing, and not pushing as hard as would be typical of you.

Environmental/Role Adjustments (ERAs).How Co-Workers See You:
Randy, your survey responses indicate that you are flexing your behaviors in order to perform your job and/or meet the demands of your environment. Because your on-the-job behaviors are not the same as your Primary Profile, your co-workers may view you quite differently than how you view yourself. The following paragraph describes how your co-workers and manager(s) may see you when you are adjusting to the pressures of your job:



Randy, your co-workers may observe that you are very patient, steady, cooperative, and dependable. Your highly creative and innovative nature is especially apparent within your area of expertise. You are quite persistent in achieving results, and, at times, you may use a demanding manner. Your good sense of timing and capacity to develop systematic methods enhances your ability to realize long-range planning if called upon to do so. While you communicate in a friendly manner, you tend to be a teller rather than a persuader. You prefer small groups and one-on-one relationships.

Stress Level.Intro: Stress is not necessarily all bad; some stress can be healthy. The Stress Level measurement indicates how well you are handling your current environmental demands. The "Good" response indicates that you are coping with your environment effectively, while the other responses indicate varying degrees of difficulty in dealing with it.
Stress Level.Description: Randy, your responses indicate that during the above-mentioned period, your stress level was Very Stressed. This indicates that you sometimes felt overwhelmed by the challenges in your environment. You may have desired more support from your colleagues, or for more time to get things done.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Energy Level measures capacity for activity, and Energy can be thought of as an individual's "battery power." Energy might be depleted at an increased rate while operating within a stressful environment or in a managerial role. Food, sleep, and relaxation can recharge one's Energy.
When energy runs out, the following symptoms tend to appear:
Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).
Energy Level.Description: Randy, you currently have a Very High Energy Level. You are highly effective in demanding environments. If called upon, you can maintain your effectiveness in the face of longer hours and stress-related situations. You are also able to juggle a multitude of tasks, or can intensely focus on a single task as necessary.

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Randy, your responses indicate that you are constantly on the lookout for new opportunities. Others are likely to sense that you have a special gift for seeing what is really important, and that you possess a sense of mission. Because of these attributes, people tend to see you as an inspirational leader. Your ease with people and your political savvy help you to distinguish yourself and to add real value to your organization.
You see more clearly than others the transformational possibilities inherent in current circumstances. Because your grand ambitions are supported by your often breakthrough thinking, you feel that you possess the wherewithal to make even the impossible happen. You enjoy spending time alone, contemplating the revision of existing processes, or designing new products or services.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Randy, your responses indicate that you particularly enjoy social situations in which you feel that you can be yourself. Even in your work environment, you do not mind displaying your personal attitudes and dispositions. You are more likely than most people to be fully committed to your current work relationships. Your internal state often influences your behavior.
You work best when given broad authority to complete tasks in the most expedient way possible. You prefer to focus on the task at hand, and not on deciphering other people's emotional cues.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Pragmatist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Randy, your responses indicate that you prefer a "hands-on" approach to learning that involves active experimentation. You learn best by engaging in group discussions, homework, or other projects. You tend to dislike passive learning situations such as lectures. Because you learn best by doing, not by watching or listening, it is likely that you thoroughly practice new skills until you have mastered them.

You enjoy testing theories to see if they work in the "real world"; and, you consistently think of better, more practical ways to get things done. You see problems as opportunities, and you frequently solve problems by trial and error. In discussions, you tend to get straight to the point; and, you prefer that discussions include only people who can contribute meaningfully. You probably spend less time than others preparing for classes or examinations.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Randy, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Tactile Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Anthony, your responses indicate that you are very thorough, precise, steady, cooperative and methodical in your approach to life and work. You exercise caution when it pertains to new people, systems and procedures. Your patient, non-rushed and conscientious style is qualified by your insistence that tasks are completed correctly. You are very loyal to people whom you respect. Criticism that is launched in your direction may produce a sensitive reaction on your part.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Exceptionally systematic and disciplined
Inspires and challenges others to excellence
Committed to high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Anthony, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in an environment that allows you to develop or use systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project.

You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly altered or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which causes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with intense disapproval; and you may be compelled to counteract by overpowering the opposition with appropriate facts or rules.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Systematic | Emphasize Quality | Doubtful | Loyal | Conscientious | Cautious | Careful | A Perfectionist
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: You try to accomplish all of your projects in a thorough and accurate manner. You may hesitate to institute change, until it is proven that it is an advancement upon the existing course of action. It is likely that you are technically oriented and primarily interested in your own specialty. You possess a strong sense of duty. Since being right is very important to you, it is possible that you may react uneasily to judgments of a critical nature.

Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: You are able to work alone and sometimes may even prefer it. It is likely that you have close ties with your family and a few intimate friends. You prefer small groups and one-on-one relationships.

Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You are able to cope with routine situations, and you approach new procedures with caution. When change and haste are initiated into your environment, they must be justified in order to be accepted by you. You are extremely dependable and steady. You prefer to work in a harmonious environment.

Interaction4.Descriptive Words: Analytical | Direct | Creative | Candid | Would Rather Tell Than Persuade
Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: If you are involved in sales related activities, it is possible that you subscribe to the position that the customer is not always right. You may enjoy participating in projects that require technical innovations.

Motivational Needs.Primary Motivators:
Because your High Trait is Conformity, you will need some of the following factors in your environment in order to be self-motivated and highly productive:
A structured environment that has few sudden or abrupt changes.
The security of basic benefits.
A worked out system and quality products.
Praise for specific accomplishments.
Standard operating procedures and sound actions.
Fairness in dealings with individuals and groups.
Time to do things right.
Motivational Needs.Primary Demotivators:
In contrast, you are likely to be demotivated when:
You are constantly criticized.
The rules are changed without plenty of notice.
There is a lack of procedures, quality and fairness.
There is not a worked out system.
Motivational Needs.Other Motivators:
These motivators are based on the other three traits: because your Dominance trait is lower
There is strong, capable leadership in your environment.
Direction as to what is to be done and when.
A predictable environment that affords a significant amount of protection and peace.
because your Extroversion trait is lower
A limited amount of emotional exposure.
Time alone to think creatively and figure things out.
Respected as a person and taken seriously.
Socializing in a circle where people are known one-on-one.
Time to prepare for group presentations.
because your Patience trait is higher
A stable and harmonious working environment.
A minimum of personality conflicts.
Adequate time to adjust.
A limited number of last minute time pressures.

How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Anthony, your responses indicate that you tend to favor the Rational approach to decision-making. You approach the decision-making process carefully, capably, and calmly. It is likely that your skill at organization enables you to conduct a steady, thorough analysis of any problem.

Leadership Style.Intro: The range of Leadership Styles is:Authoritative - Persuasive - Mentor - Traditionalist - Adaptable
Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by managing your people through systems and procedures. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while making sure that projects are handled correctly. You show respect for colleagues' positions and you expect the same in return. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to plan and design systems and procedures. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one. You pace yourself and your people well in order to reach your goals.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Anthony, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You pursue your goals energetically, and you persevere until your goals are achieved. You strive continually for self-improvement, and you are not discouraged by temporary setbacks or disappointments. Your negotiation, persuasion, leadership, and conflict resolution skills are probably very well developed. You perform your work conscientiously, and delay gratification while pursuing your goals. You display appropriate self-confidence. You tend to understand the emotional makeup of others.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Theorist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. Anthony, your responses indicate that you prefer an analytical, conceptual approach to learning. You tend to rely heavily on logical thinking and rational evaluation. You probably tend to be more oriented towards things and symbols, and less towards other people. You learn best in authority-directed, impersonal learning situations that emphasize theory and systematic analysis. You probably do not enjoy unstructured "experiential learning" approaches such as group exercises and simulations.

You are a highly disciplined thinker who sees patterns and connections that other people do not. You take a step-by-step approach to problem solving, and you rely on facts, not feelings, when making decisions. Because abstract conceptualization is a particular strength, you probably find it easy to discern similarities among apparently dissimilar concepts, things, or people. You work hard to minimize uncertainty or ambiguity in your learning environment. Your instructors and fellow learners might regard you as a perfectionist.

Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Jeff, your responses indicate that you are very precise, persistent, and scholarly. You utilize a systematic approach to problem solving. Since you are concerned with the accuracy of information, you exercise skepticism and caution in regard to matters that have not been proven. High Trait.Candidate Description:
Jeff, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.

You have an impartial, careful, and contemplative approach to learning that relies heavily on your intuitive perceptions about human nature. You enjoy quietly pondering ideas and concepts, and you always seek fair solutions. Before forming an opinion, you consider all of the information available, you take your time, and you do not like to be rushed.

You tend to prefer learning situations that allow you to be an objective observer. You prefer that lecturers focus primarily on integrative conceptual models, and not on conflict and controversy. During group discussions, it is likely that you express your opinions diplomatically. In the classroom, you probably tend to keep a relatively low profile. You listen closely to what other people say, and you can probably detect meaning in their vocal tone and body language, as well.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Brad, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Theorist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should adopt the attitude that most things are more alike than they are dissimilar. Question whether or not you are thinking logically, or if you are relying overly much on your "gut feelings." Approach problem solving in a step-by-step manner. Make an effort to separate people and their personalities from the intellectual ideas and concepts they profess. If you are unclear about the details of a concept, ask questions or do additional research until the ambiguity is resolved. Focus on the distinction between facts and opinions.
Personal Learning Styles.Sensory Learning Style:
Your responses indicate that you prefer the Auditory Sensory Learning Style. You are probably already skilled at:
Listening attentively to lengthy speeches or lectures.
Understanding and following oral instructions.
Remembering things by repeating them aloud.
In order to develop a more balanced learning style, you may want to try the following:
Creating charts and graphs to make sense of complex information.
Remembering things by picturing them in your head.
Using an outline to follow along during lectures.
Remembering things by writing them down several times.
Conducting experiments that reinforce basic concepts.
Discovering how things work by taking them apart.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Sean, your responses indicate that you are very precise, and you demand conformity to what is generally accepted as right. Your inclination to be a specialist, in your field of endeavor, is advanced by your interest in systems and structure. You come across in a direct manner, and do not hesitate to express skepticism in a candid tone. You tend to be strict when it comes to following rules, and if asked to enforce them, you use an authoritative approach. Criticism may cause you to react sensitively.

High Trait.Key Points Attributed to Your High Trait:
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency
Able to manage a multitude of details
Exceptionally systematic and disciplined
Inspires and challenges others to excellence
Committed to high ethical standards
High Trait.Candidate Description:
Sean, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and skillful professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in an environment that allows you to develop or use systems, which you feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project.

You show sensitivity and responsiveness when it comes to motivating praise and demotivating criticism. Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles and actions involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are able to focus on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when solving problems or when asked to make changes.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly altered or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which causes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with intense disapproval; and you may be compelled to counteract by overpowering the opposition with appropriate facts or rules.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.

You approach the decision-making process carefully, and with a desire to avoid making mistakes. It is likely that you attempt to make decisions only after considering all the facts.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.

High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.

Profile Report.Profile Summary: Alicia, your responses indicate that you are steady, conscientious and mild-mannered. You persistently work toward achieving accuracy, and you show a great deal of interest in issues concerning what you feel is right versus wrong. You are a loyal and unassuming perfectionist who desires security and clear guidelines. Your sensitivity to criticism and caution of the new is qualified by your use of gentle persuasion and firmness when making your position known. You may prefer a few close friends rather than many casual acquaintances.

Exceptionally committed, congenial and systematic
Able to manage a multitude of details
Dedicated to maintaining highest quality and efficiency

High Trait.Candidate Description:
Alicia, in addition to the qualities above which generate the power behind your work, you are a conscientious and composed professional, who is dutifully committed to achieving exacting standards while meeting pressure and deadlines. You thrive in a temperate environment that allows you to utilize systems, which you may feel are essential to the smooth operation of a program or project. You may show sensitivity and responsiveness to motivating praise and demotivating criticism.

Because of your strong organizational skills, you can be relied upon to implement company policy and to support colleagues, as long as the principles involved are fair to all concerned.

Since you are capable of focusing on specific tasks while keeping an overall project goal in mind, you are skilled in setting priorities and like to provide your people with exact direction as to when and how a project is to be done. You may prefer to explore all options and avenues when asked to make changes or when solving problems.
High Trait.Sensitive Areas: An undependable, rapidly changing environment wherein systems are being constantly changed or disregarded; and misdirected criticism, which provokes a marked decline in the recipient's attitude and productivity.
High Trait.Potential Reactions: When an issue is dealt with in an improper manner or when criticism is unfair, you may react with moderate disapproval; and you may respond by informing the opposition of proper facts or rules directly related to the issue or criticism.

Other Traits.Intro: Your other three traits, Dominance, Extroversion, and Patience, and their location in your profile are listed in the Summary section, and they are also portrayed on the profile graph. These three traits have a direct effect on your High Trait and how it is interpreted. You will recall that the High Trait accounts for 50 to 70 percent of an individual's behavior; these other three traits account for the remaining 50 to 30 percent.

Interaction1.Descriptive Words: Good With Detail | Fussy | Sensitive To Judgment | Conservative | Formal | Cautious | Conscientious | Very Loyal | A Perfectionist
Interaction1.Summary Paragraph: The distinction between what is right and wrong is very important to you. You want to know if you are accepted in a group, because you desire the security of belonging. It is possible that you further your goals by taking one step at a time, and by envisioning that everything will turn out alright. In accomplishing your activities, you dutifully follow accepted patterns, rules and systems that you respect and feel are fair. You are not critical in public, unless a rule or principle has been broken. It is likely that you function in a rather traditional fashion.

Interaction2.Summary Paragraph: In your work environment, you prefer clearly defined direction from strong leadership, which is both fair and committed to quality.

Interaction3.Descriptive Words: Patient | Sincere | Stable | Non-judgmental | Cooperative | Unhurried
Interaction3.Summary Paragraph: You convey a calm and consistent style. When you are introduced to new material, you undertake to learn it thoroughly and deliberately. It is possible that you develop a few close friends and tend to keep some distance from others until you feel accepted.

Interaction4.Summary Paragraph: You manage to get matters completed, according to your preferred course of action, by using persistent and subtle persuasion. You endeavor to be fair to people and try to prevent embarrassing situations. You relate friendliness in a quiet and reassuring manner.

Decision Making Style.Intro:
How you make decisions is influenced not only by your work environment and job responsibilities, but also by your individual personality characteristics.
One common approach to decision-making -- the Rational Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on observing and analyzing concrete facts. Another common approach -- the Intuitive Decision-Making Style -- relies heavily on a person's intangible "gut feelings." Neither approach should be considered consistently superior to the other. Most people employ a combination of both approaches when they make decisions.
Decision Making Style.Description: Alicia, your responses indicate that you tend to favor the Intuitive approach to decision-making. You approach decision-making with optimism, and with only moderate anxiety about the possibility of making mistakes. You tend to be receptive to new ideas and alternative solutions.

Leadership Style.Description: You expertly perform your leadership role by managing your people through systems and procedures, and requiring them to follow your organization's rules. You exercise a conservative and watchful style while making sure that projects are handled correctly. You show respect for colleagues' positions and you expect the same in return. You may desire time alone to think matters through and to plan and design systems and procedures. You may prefer to deal with your subordinates one-on-one. You pace yourself and your people well in order to reach your goals.

Emotional Intelligence.Description: Alicia, your responses indicate that your Emotional Intelligence is exceptionally well developed. You tend to understand the emotional makeup of others, and to accurately sense what other people are feeling. Because you find it easy to see the world from another person's perspective, it is likely that you associate with a diverse group of people. You work for reasons beyond money or status. It is likely that you are proficient in both maintaining relationships and networking. You think before speaking, and form carefully considered judgments about people or situations. Furthermore, you are aware of the impact of your emotions on others.

Criteria1.Title: Personal Insight
Criteria1.Intro: The capacity to accurately recognize and understand own emotions as they occur & to base personal self-confidence on an accurate assessment of own abilities.

Criteria2.Title: Self-Discipline
Criteria2.Intro: The capacity to delay gratification when pursuing goals, to speak carefully, & to control negative impulses.

Criteria3.Title: Drive
Criteria3.Intro: The capacity to pursue goals energetically, to relentlessly seek self-improvement, & to persevere despite obstacles or disappointments.

Criteria4.Title: Interpersonal Insight
Criteria4.Intro: The capacity to understand the emotions of others, to adjust own style to interact well with others, & to empathize with alternative perspectives.

Criteria5.Title: Social Agility
Criteria5.Intro: The capacity to be at ease in almost any social situation, to prevent or resolve conflict, to cultivate rapport & build lasting relationships.

Profile Report.Conscientiousness: You recognize the importance of self-discipline, careful planning, and thoroughness in completing tasks. Your above-average ability to completely fulfill specific goals and obligations is complemented by a usually positive sense of motivation.

Adjustment1.Description: This indicates that you are exerting more control, assertiveness and forcefulness than is natural for you. This might be caused by the pressures in your environment.

Adjustment2.Description: This indicates that events are not happening as quickly as you desire. Perhaps deadlines are not being met, and your sense of urgency is increasing with a need to move at a faster pace.

Environmental/Role Adjustments (ERAs).How Co-Workers See You:
Alicia, your survey responses indicate that you are flexing your behaviors in order to perform your job and/or meet the demands of your environment. Because your on-the-job behaviors are not the same as your Primary Profile, your co-workers may view you quite differently than how you view yourself. The following paragraph describes how your co-workers and manager(s) may see you when you are adjusting to the pressures of your job:



Alicia, your co-workers may observe that you are personable, yet demanding when matters need to be completed correctly. Your convincing and authoritative tone leads you to exhibit an attitude that exudes control. Your intense, competitive, and goal-oriented nature causes you to push ahead to achieve results. You possess social composure and the ability to think quickly during public exposure. Your enjoyment of contact with people is heightened if you are in control of the communication. Criticism may cause you to respond in a sensitive manner.

Stress Level.Intro: Stress is not necessarily all bad; some stress can be healthy. The Stress Level measurement indicates how well you are handling your current environmental demands. The "Good" response indicates that you are coping with your environment effectively, while the other responses indicate varying degrees of difficulty in dealing with it.
Stress Level.Description: Alicia, your responses indicate that during the above-mentioned period, your stress level was Mildly Stressed. This indicates that your environment was of some concern to you, probably because goals were not being achieved quickly enough. It may also indicate that you felt that the rewards gained did not justify the effort expended.

Energy Level.Intro:
Energy Level indicates an individual's current degree of vigor, alertness, and responsiveness. Energy Level measures capacity for activity, and Energy can be thought of as an individual's "battery power." Energy might be depleted at an increased rate while operating within a stressful environment or in a managerial role. Food, sleep, and relaxation can recharge one's Energy.
When energy runs out, the following symptoms tend to appear:
Increased susceptibility to accidents and mental errors.
Reduced ability to concentrate.
Reversion from Public Self (Environmental/Role Adjustment) to Basic Self (the Primary Profile).
Energy Level.Description: Alicia, you currently have a Very High Energy Level. You are highly effective in demanding environments. If called upon, you can maintain your effectiveness in the face of longer hours and stress-related situations. You are also able to juggle a multitude of tasks, or can intensely focus on a single task as necessary.

Proactivity.Intro: The Proactive Personality Scale measures how likely an individual is to intentionally and actively create positive change in the work environment. High proactive personalities identify opportunities and act on them; they show initiative, take action, and persevere until they bring about meaningful change. They will often seek ways to improve the work environment as necessary to facilitate effective job performance. Individuals lower on the Proactive Personality Scale will exhibit these behavioral tendencies less regularly.
Proactivity.Description:
Alicia, your responses indicate that you are highly aware of the legal and ethical boundaries concerning business behavior. You ensure that all of your actions are strategically sound, politically appropriate, and tactically feasible. For you to support change, it must be clearly defined and proven constructive. As a manager, you tend to make a great effort to ensure that nothing goes wrong. When faced with unfamiliar circumstances, you tend to rely on proven approaches to see you through.
You tend to act decisively to defend your independence. It is likely that you are less concerned with performance standards imposed by others. You may have a tendency to rebel when others are overly judgmental or critical of your unconventional methods.

Self-Monitoring.Intro: The Self-Monitoring Scale measures two dimensions of an individual's personality: Behavioral Flexibility and Career Mobility. High Self-Monitors usually find it easy to "flex" their workplace behaviors in order to adapt to specific situations or individuals. High Self-Monitors are comfortable performing a wide variety of roles, and are skilled at impression management and interpersonal communication. High Self-Monitors are more likely to actively seek promotions, and to follow opportunity to different departments, companies or industries. A High Self-Monitor is more likely than others to relocate geographically in pursuit of opportunity. An individual's Self-Monitoring rating does not predict technical ability or performance on job-related tasks.
Self-Monitoring.Description:
Alicia, your responses indicate that you are committed and principled in your approach to work. You are less responsive than other people to shifting environmental demands. You tend to behave consistently even in stressful situations. It may frustrate you that such consistency is not regularly recognized or rewarded.
You value the freedom to pursue work that is compatible to your own interests. You work best when supervisors provide guidance but refrain from micro-managing.
The behavioral adjustments you are making in response to the demands of your current environment are within the normal range. Any additional demands might begin to negatively impact your effectiveness.

Personal Learning Styles.Intro: The four main learning styles are Activist, Reflector, Theorist, and Pragmatist. The Activist engages enthusiastically in new experiences. The Reflector prefers quietly gathering and pondering information. The Theorist enjoys abstract concepts and logical analysis. The Pragmatist uses a trial-and-error approach to problem solving, and prefers that learning have practical applications.
Personal Learning Styles.Description:
Your primary learning style preference is: Theorist. It is highly probable that you also employ other learning strategies in addition to your primary style, depending on the educational context. The relative strengths of your learning styles are presented in the graph at the bottom of this section. The following profile summary describes your preferred approach to learning.
Alicia, your responses indicate that you prefer an analytical, conceptual approach to learning. You tend to rely heavily on logical thinking and rational evaluation. You probably tend to be more oriented towards things and symbols, and less towards other people. You learn best in authority-directed, impersonal learning situations that emphasize theory and systematic analysis. You probably do not enjoy unstructured "experiential learning" approaches such as group exercises and simulations.

You are a highly disciplined thinker who sees patterns and connections that other people do not. You take a step-by-step approach to problem solving, and you rely on facts, not feelings, when making decisions. Because abstract conceptualization is a particular strength, you probably find it easy to discern similarities among apparently dissimilar concepts, things, or people. You work hard to minimize uncertainty or ambiguity in your learning environment. Your instructors and fellow learners might regard you as a perfectionist.
Personal Learning Styles.Developmental Suggestions: Alicia, your responses indicate that you tend to prefer the Activist learning style less than other styles. In order to develop an integrated learning strategy, you should emphasize your critical thinking skills when listening to lectures or presentations. Employ a healthy dose of skepticism even when learning from "experts." Always seek out evidence that supports (or contradicts) the assertions made by others. Contribute to class discussions and ask questions. Volunteer to participate in experiments or demonstrations.
